questions.q: Which kingdom did Drupada rule?
questions.explanation: Drupada was king of Panchala, the great realm south of the Ganga and longtime rival of the Kurus.

questions.q: Who was Drupada's boyhood friend who later became his most bitter enemy?
questions.explanation: Drupada and Drona were close friends as students, but Drupada's insult to the impoverished Drona turned them into lifelong enemies.

questions.q: Which daughter of Drupada was born from a sacrificial fire?
questions.explanation: Draupadi emerged dark and dazzling from the same sacrificial fire that produced her brother Dhrishtadyumna.

questions.q: Which son of Drupada was destined from birth to kill Drona?
questions.explanation: Dhrishtadyumna stepped out of Drupada's sacrificial fire already armed, born expressly for the death of Drona.

questions.q: Why did Drupada commission a great fire sacrifice?
options: To win back lost territory from Magadha
To beg the gods for rain in Panchala
To crown himself emperor of the north
To obtain a son who would slay Drona
questions.explanation: Burning with humiliation after his defeat, Drupada sought a son who could kill Drona, and the rite gave him both a son and a daughter.

questions.q: What did Drona demand from the Kuru princes as his teaching fee?
options: The capture of Drupada in battle
A herd of ten thousand cows
A celestial bow from the gods
questions.explanation: As guru-dakshina Drona asked his royal students to defeat Drupada and bring him captive, which they did.

questions.q: Which warrior defeated and captured Drupada to pay Drona's teaching fee?
questions.explanation: After the Kauravas failed, Arjuna stormed Kampilya, defeated Drupada, and brought him bound before Drona.

questions.q: What event did Drupada hold to find a husband for his daughter Draupadi?
options: A swayamvara with an archery test
questions.explanation: Drupada held a swayamvara whose near-impossible archery test required piercing the eye of a fish revolving overhead.

questions.q: By what other name was Drupada known, from which Draupadi's epithet Yajnaseni derives?
questions.explanation: Drupada was also called Yajnasena, and his fire-born daughter was therefore known as Yajnaseni.

questions.q: On which side did Drupada fight in the Kurukshetra war?
questions.explanation: As father-in-law of the Pandavas and enemy of Drona, Drupada brought his Panchala forces to the Pandava side.

questions.q: Where did Drupada and Drona become friends as boys?
options: At the hermitage of the sage Bharadwaja
questions.explanation: Drupada was sent to study at the ashram of Drona's father, the sage Bharadwaja, where the two boys grew inseparable.

questions.q: What did young Drupada promise Drona during their student days?
options: Half his kingdom when he became king
A seat beside him at every sacrifice
questions.explanation: The boy Drupada promised Drona half his kingdom, a pledge he mocked when Drona later came to claim friendship.

questions.q: How did King Drupada respond when the impoverished Drona came to him claiming friendship?
options: He declared that friendship exists only between equals
He granted him a village and a herd of cattle
He quietly paid him to leave Panchala forever
He imprisoned him for insolence before the court
questions.explanation: Drupada scorned the poor brahmin, saying a king and a beggar cannot be friends, an insult Drona never forgot.

questions.q: After defeating Drupada, how did Drona divide Panchala?
options: He gave the whole kingdom to Arjuna
He annexed all of it to Hastinapura
He razed the capital and left it kingless
He kept the northern half and returned the southern half
questions.explanation: Drona spared Drupada's life but kept Panchala north of the Ganga for himself, returning the southern half so they would now be equals.

questions.q: What was the capital of the southern Panchala that remained to Drupada?
questions.explanation: Drupada ruled his diminished kingdom from Kampilya, where Draupadi's swayamvara was later held.

questions.q: Which brahmin brothers conducted the sacrifice that produced Drupada's fire-born children?
questions.explanation: The brothers Yaja and Upayaja performed the rite from which Dhrishtadyumna and Draupadi were born.

questions.q: Who was Drupada's father?
questions.explanation: Drupada was the son of King Prishata of Panchala, which is why he is also called Parshata.

questions.q: From which capital did Drona rule the northern half of Panchala he seized?
questions.explanation: Drona took Ahichchhatra as the seat of northern Panchala, leaving Drupada the south with Kampilya.

questions.q: Why did Drupada secretly design Draupadi's swayamvara test to be nearly impossible?
options: He hoped only Arjuna could pass it and become his ally
He wanted no suitor to ever claim his daughter
He intended Karna to win and rule Panchala
He wished to humiliate the kings of the eastern realms
questions.explanation: Drupada longed for Arjuna, the one warrior able to counter Drona, and shaped the bow test with him in mind despite rumors of the Pandavas' death.

questions.q: Drupada objected to Draupadi marrying all five Pandavas; who persuaded him to consent?
questions.explanation: Vyasa revealed Draupadi's past-life boon and divine destiny, convincing Drupada the five-husband marriage was sanctioned by fate.

questions.q: Which child of Drupada was the princess Amba reborn?
questions.explanation: Amba was reborn as Drupada's child Shikhandi, raised as a son and destined to be the instrument of Bhishma's fall.

questions.q: Who killed Drupada during the Kurukshetra war?
questions.explanation: Drona slew both Drupada and Virata on the fifteenth day of the war, shortly before his own death at Dhrishtadyumna's hands.

questions.q: What happened when Drupada's queen was summoned to receive the sacrificial offering meant to give her children?
options: She was unprepared, so the oblation was poured straight into the fire
She refused it, fearing the prophecy of Drona's death
She fainted at the altar and was carried away
She passed it secretly to her serving maid
questions.explanation: The queen asked the sages to wait as she was not ritually ready, but Yaja poured the offering into the fire, from which the children then arose.

questions.q: Which sage first refused Drupada's request to perform the vengeance sacrifice, directing him elsewhere?
questions.explanation: The scrupulous Upayaja rejected Drupada's offer of wealth and sent him to his elder brother Yaja, who accepted the task.

questions.q: How did Drupada spend the years after Drona seized half his kingdom?
options: Raising mercenary armies in the western hills
Performing penance to Shiva in the Himalayas
Searching for brahmins able to grant him a son who could kill Drona
Building alliances through marriages with Magadha
questions.explanation: Knowing no warrior could match Drona, Drupada wandered among brahmin settlements seeking priests with the power to give him a destined son.

questions.q: What did the disembodied voice proclaim when Dhrishtadyumna stepped from Drupada's sacrificial fire?
options: That he would rule all of Bharata
That he would betray his own father
That he would die before his sister
That he had been born for the destruction of Drona
questions.explanation: A voice from the sky announced that the fire-born prince would dispel Panchala's grief by slaying Drona.

questions.q: What command did Drupada hold in the Pandava war effort?
options: He led one of the seven akshauhinis of the Pandava host
He commanded only the cavalry reserves
He guarded the camp and the supply lines
He served as an elder counselor without troops
questions.explanation: Drupada was named one of the seven leaders, each commanding a full akshauhini of the Pandava army.

questions.q: Who avenged Drupada's death on the very day he fell?
options: Shikhandi, with arrows through Drona's chest
questions.explanation: On the fifteenth day Dhrishtadyumna beheaded Drona, fulfilling his birth destiny and avenging his father within hours of Drupada's fall.

questions.q: By what clan names were Drupada's people also known in the epic?
questions.explanation: The Panchalas of Drupada's house are repeatedly called Somakas and Srinjayas after their famed ancestors.

questions.q: What diplomatic step did Drupada propose at the Pandava war council in Upaplavya?
options: Offering Duryodhana the whole of Panchala for peace
Challenging Drona to single combat before the war
Asking Balarama to arbitrate between the cousins
Sending his own priest to Hastinapura while swiftly gathering allies
questions.explanation: Drupada advised dispatching messengers at once to allied kings and sent his family priest to the Kuru court as the first peace envoy.
